name: ofc-utility
description: OFC option-value re-ranker MVP (RESEARCH.md §PFC.OFC). When --rerank-utility is set, results are re-sorted by utility = score * (0.5 + 0.5 * strength) * (1 - cost_factor) where cost_factor = min(0.3, tokens / 10000). Without the flag, the long verbose memory (high token cost) appears in top-3 because BM25 score alone outranks the shorter lengthy memory. With the flag, the longer verbose memory's cost penalty (~30%) drops it below the lengthy memory whose penalty is only ~8%, so lengthy enters top-3 and verbose drops out. MVP scope: cost-driven flip is the demonstrable signal. The strength dimension is present in the formula but uniform across fresh memories (all strength=1.0 at t=0), so its effect requires time-decay or future outcome-driven tests.
